flashcards.front: What is a binary tree traversal?
flashcards.back: Traversal is the process of visiting all nodes of a binary tree in a systematic manner. Each node is visited exactly once, producing a linear sequence of nodes.
Nodes are visited in order: Node → Left Subtree → Right Subtree. Used for creating copy of tree, prefix expression of expression trees.
Nodes are visited in order: Left Subtree → Node → Right Subtree. For BST, produces sorted output. Used for inorder successor/predecessor.
Nodes are visited in order: Left Subtree → Right Subtree → Node. Used for deleting tree, postfix expression of expression trees.
Nodes are visited level by level from left to right. Uses queue data structure. Also called Breadth-First Search (BFS) for trees.
O(n) where n is number of nodes - each node is visited exactly once.
O(h) where h is height of tree. In worst case (skewed tree), h = n, so O(n). In balanced tree, h = log n.

flashcards.front: Why use stack in iterative traversal?
flashcards.back: Stack simulates the recursive call stack, storing nodes to be visited later. It allows us to traverse without function call overhead.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between stack and queue usage in traversals?
flashcards.back: Stack is used for depth-first (pre/in/post-order) - LIFO. Queue is used for breadth-first (level-order) - FIFO.
A tree traversal technique that uses no extra space (O(1)) by modifying the tree temporarily. Creates temporary links to ancestors.
A binary tree where null pointers are replaced with threads pointing to inorder predecessor/successor. Allows inorder traversal without stack/recursion.
Used for deleting/freed tree nodes (post-order: children before parent), evaluating postfix expressions, computing directory sizes.
Inorder traversal of BST always produces nodes in non-decreasing (sorted) order. Used for sorted output, finding kth smallest element.
Traverses left boundary, leaves, and right boundary of tree in anticlockwise order. Used for printing tree outline.
Nodes with same slope (same parent-right-child pattern) are visited together. Creates groups of nodes at 45-degree angles.
